COMMANDS

result.stdout:
CYCODT - AI-powered CLI Test Framework, Version 1.0.0-alpha-20250526.1
Copyright(c) 2025, Rob Chambers. All rights reserved.

CYCODT EXPECT

  The cycodt expect manages LLM and regex expectations.
  
USAGE: cycodt expect check [...]
   OR: cycodt expect format [...]

EXAMPLES

  EXAMPLE 1: Use AI to check the output of a command

    git status | cycodt expect check --instructions "branch should be 'main'"

  EXAMPLE 2: Save strict regex from a command; use it to checkout output

    git status | cycodt expect format --save-output git-status-clean.txt
    git status | cycodt expect check --regex-file git-status-clean.txt

SEE ALSO

  cycodt help expect check
  cycodt help expect format
  cycodt help usage

CYCODT - AI-powered CLI Test Framework, Version 1.0.0-alpha-20250526.1
Copyright(c) 2025, Rob Chambers. All rights reserved.

CYCODT EXPECT

  The cycodt expect manages LLM and regex expectations.
  
USAGE: cycodt expect check [...]
   OR: cycodt expect format [...]

EXAMPLES

  EXAMPLE 1: Use AI to check the output of a command

    git status | cycodt expect check --instructions "branch should be 'main'"

  EXAMPLE 2: Save strict regex from a command; use it to checkout output

    git status | cycodt expect format --save-output git-status-clean.txt
    git status | cycodt expect check --regex-file git-status-clean.txt

SEE ALSO

  cycodt help expect format
  cycodt help usage

result.stdout:
CYCODT - AI-powered CLI Test Framework, Version 1.0.0-alpha-20250526.1
Copyright(c) 2025, Rob Chambers. All rights reserved.

Invalid argument: -h

CYCODT EXPECT CHECK

  The cycodt expect check command validates input against specified expectations,
  similar to running a test case but directly from the command line.

USAGE: cycodt expect check [...]

  EXPECTATIONS

    --regex @FILE         Read regex patterns from FILE (one per line)
    --regex PATTERN       Regex pattern(s) that should match (can specify multiple)
    
    --not-regex @FILE     Read not-regex patterns from FILE (one per line)
    --not-regex PATTERN   Regex pattern(s) that should NOT match (can specify multiple)
    
    --instructions TEXT   LLM instructions for checking output
    --instructions @FILE  File containing LLM instructions

  INPUT/OUTPUT

    --input FILE          Read input from FILE
    --input -             Read input from stdin (default)

    --save-output FILE    Write check results to FILE

SEE ALSO

  cycodt help expect examples
  cycodt help expect format
  cycodt help usage

result.stdout:
CYCODT - AI-powered CLI Test Framework, Version 1.0.0-alpha-20250526.1
Copyright(c) 2025, Rob Chambers. All rights reserved.

Invalid argument: -h

CYCODT EXPECT FORMAT

  The cycodt expect format command transforms input text into properly formatted 
  regex patterns for use in the expect-regex field of YAML test files.

USAGE: cycodt expect format [...]

  INPUT/OUTPUT

    --input FILE            Read input from FILE
    --input -               Read input from stdin (default)

    --save-output FILE      Write output to FILE instead of stdout

  FORMAT OPTIONS

    --strict [TRUE/FALSE]   Whether to use strict regex formatting (default: true)

SEE ALSO

  cycodt help expect examples
  cycodt help expect check
  cycodt help usage
